message:
Eliminate memory leak in Azure provider role name handling

The previous to_static_role() function used Box::leak() to convert
unknown role names into &'static str, permanently leaking heap memory
for every non-standard role string encountered. While rare in practice
(only triggered by exotic compat overrides), this was an architectural
hazard that could compound in long-running sessions.

Replace with normalize_role() -> String:
- Known roles (system, developer, user, assistant, tool, function) are
  returned directly with zero allocation overhead on the hot path
- Unknown roles get case-insensitive matching as a fallback before
  preserving the original value verbatim
- Change AzureMessage.role from &'static str to String throughout
- Add tests verifying both normalization of known roles with odd casing
  and preservation of genuinely unknown custom role names

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
